headline: Record Renewable Energy Capacity Added in 2025
meta_description: The world added 692 GW of renewable energy capacity in 2025, the fastest expansion rate ever recorded and equivalent to India's entire power capacity.
summary_short: Global renewable energy capacity hit a historic record in 2025, adding more power than an entire major nation produces—and growth continues accelerating.
body:
In 2025, the world achieved a historic milestone: 692 gigawatts of new renewable energy capacity came online, surpassing all previous records. To grasp the scale of this achievement, that figure exceeds the entire power generation capacity of India, one of the world's most populous nations.

This record represents not a one-time spike but the latest acceleration in a sustained upward trend. Wind, solar, hydroelectric, and other renewable sources are now being deployed faster than ever before, driven by falling technology costs, improving grid infrastructure, and growing commitment from governments and businesses worldwide.

The implications are far-reaching. Each gigawatt of renewable capacity replaces fossil fuels that would otherwise generate electricity, reducing greenhouse gas emissions and improving air quality. For communities facing energy poverty, these expanding renewable grids offer pathways to electricity access without the environmental burden of coal or oil dependency. This acceleration demonstrates that a global transition to clean energy is not merely possible—it is already happening at unprecedented speed.
